title: How using a VPS made my trading way easier
author: EndlessKnight_154
selftext:
I used to trade straight from my PC at home, and honestly, it was way more stressful than I expected. One time I had a perfect setup on gold, and right as price hit my entry, my internet dropped for a few minutes. By the time it came back, I had missed the move entirely and ended up frustrated. There were other times when a Windows update kicked in or my PC restarted at the worst possible moment. It felt like I was constantly fighting tech issues instead of the market.

A few months ago I finally decided to switch to a VPS, and it’s been a total game changer. My platform runs 24/7 without me having to babysit it, so I can focus on spotting setups, analyzing price action, and journaling my trades instead of worrying about whether I’ll get disconnected mid-trade. It’s such a relief just knowing everything stays online no matter what happens at home.

I’m curious how other traders use VPS. Are you mostly running EAs or bots, or do you just use it to keep your platform stable? Would love to hear how it’s helped you stay consistent.

comments.author: ddchbr
comments.body: But if your local system drops how would you even access your VPS? From mobile?

comments.author: UseUseAccount
